domain: pedology / soil science (soil horizon emergence via differential weathering)
specific_mechanism: Soil horizons (O, A, E, B, C, R) emerge by translocation, accumulation, leaching, and transformation operating at different depths over decades-to-millennia. The stratification is not pre-designed but emerges from differential weathering processes interacting; horizon boundaries are sharp despite the gradual nature of the process
llm_application: An asynchronous multi-agent communication pool where agents do not coordinate explicitly but each operates with a specific 'translocation/accumulation/leaching/transformation' role over the shared knowledge substrate; horizons (knowledge strata at different specificity levels) emerge from the role differentiation without explicit communication or central planner. The communication is encoded only via what each agent leaves in or removes from the shared substrate.
novelty_claim: Existing asynchronous multi-agent communication uses message passing or shared blackboards. No published 2024-2026 LLM/AI multi-agent work formalizes a soil-horizon-emergence-style indirect coordination via 4-role differential modification of a shared substrate that produces sharp emergent strata.
